app.name: pinact
app.one_liner: A CLI tool to pin GitHub Actions and reusable workflows to immutable commit hashes for improved security and reliability.

screens.name: Main CLI Interface
screens.purpose: Provide command-line access to pin, update, verify, and review GitHub Actions and reusable workflows.
primary_actions: Create pull request reviews with pinned versions
Generate diff output for version changes
Run pinning operation on workflow files

rust_commands.name: create_review
rust_commands.purpose: Generate a pull request review comment showing changes made by pinning.

rust_commands.name: pin_versions
rust_commands.purpose: Pin GitHub Actions and reusable workflows in workflow files to their latest commit hashes.

rust_commands.name: run_diff_check
rust_commands.purpose: Run pinact in diff mode with check flag to validate without modifying files.

rust_commands.name: update_versions
rust_commands.purpose: Update already-pinned actions to newer versions based on configuration.

rust_commands.name: verify_annotations
rust_commands.purpose: Check that version annotations in workflow files match the actual pinned commits.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add support for reusable workflows
week 1: Implement core pinning logic for GitHub Actions
week 1: Integrate diff generation and verification modes
acceptance_tests: Configuration options such as version separator must be respected from both config file and CLI flags.
Integration with ghtkn must allow secure token usage when enabled via environment variable.
Running `pinact run --diff --check` should display appropriate log levels and error messages when validation fails.
The tool must correctly replace action tags like `actions/checkout@v3` with commit hashes and retain version annotations (e.g., `# v3.5.1`).
